name: BARRETT ESOPHAGUS
definition: BARRETT SYNDROME progressive replacement of stratified squamous epithelium by metaplastic columnar

demographics.Age: 0-15 years and 40-88 years (mean of 55 years); M > F; mainly among Whites

discriminating_features: fine reticular / granular mucosal pattern (3-30%) resembling areae gastricae of the stomach
netlike web of thin linear / intersecting barium-filled grooves or crevices surrounding
large deep wide-mouthed peptic ulcer (= Barrett ulcer) at upwardly displaced

general: fine reticular / granular mucosal pattern (3-30%) resembling areae gastricae of the stomach
netlike web of thin linear / intersecting barium-filled grooves or crevices surrounding
large deep wide-mouthed peptic ulcer (= Barrett ulcer) at upwardly displaced
several-cm-long stricture (71%) in lower esophagus (60%) or upper to midesophagus
ringlike constrictions / smooth tapered segments of concentric narrowing in
plaquelike / focal irregularity / nodularity / sessile polyps
